source: Closed FDA warning-letter carrier pack drafted from carrier_contract_registry.py; vocabulary only, not facts.
purpose: Constrain FDA warning-letter compiles to a small governed carrier set for warning-letter wrapper, facility identity, chronology, violations, citations, response requirements, consultant recommendation, conclusion scope, and omission accountability.

lenses.purpose: Document wrapper, facility identity, and correspondence parties.
Inspection, Form 483 response, prior-warning-letter, and meeting chronology.
Numbered violations, CFR citations, and atomic violation details.
Source-stated 501(a)(2)(A) insanitary-condition observations or examples distinct from CGMP violation lists.
Response requirements and consultant recommendations. Consultant qualification citations require a narrower citation-specific lens.
FDA critique of firm responses or corrective actions attached to numbered CGMP items.
FDA critique that submitted response documentation, protocol, record, evidence, or validation material was not provided.
FDA critique that a response failed to investigate, failed to ensure a thorough investigation, or left a CGMP discrepancy uninvestigated.
Tier 1 structural slots for numbered CGMP violation details without open detail values.
Conclusion scope, not-all-inclusive language, recurrence responsibility, and accountable omissions.

predicates.notes: Identifies one source-stated FDA warning letter. Do not hide violations, response instructions, or conclusion text in wrapper slots.

Links one regulated facility to compact name, location, and identifier values such as FEI. Use not_stated when the facility is stated but no facility identifier is stated; do not use warning-letter, WL, MARCS-CMS, CMS, or registration-class values as facility identifiers.

Links a letter to recipient, issuing office, signatory, contact, or responsible official roles without inferring responsibility. Prefer the regulated firm/entity as recipient when stated; use named people only for stated roles. For contact, prefer a source-stated correspondence email/mailbox when no named contact person is stated for that channel.

Preserves one source-stated FDA inspection event with typed start and end dates. The inspecting_body slot is the agency/body that performed the inspection; do not fill it with the warning-letter issuing office header.
Links a source-stated Form FDA 483 response to its inspection and response date.

Preserves a source-stated prior warning letter and compact relation or status. Use the stated addressee/scope. Do not use this for prior inspections, citations, trends, deficiencies, or repeated observations.
Preserves a source-stated FDA meeting or teleconference date.
Preserves one numbered violation with a controlled compact category, never a paragraph summary.
Preserves one numbered 501(a)(2)(B) CGMP violation item and its exact CGMP requirement citation before deterministic category population.
Links one violation or letter-level subject to an exact compact citation atom.

Preserves one atomic violation detail. Record-review failures should use record_review_subject or missing_record_type, not observation_subject. Observation subjects are for observed evidence supporting facility/equipment/control violations.

Preserves the governed structural slot of a violation detail without claiming the open detail_value. This is useful as Tier 1 structural coverage when detail values are unstable; it is not an answer substitute for value-level questions.

Preserves a compact FDA critique of a firm's response, corrective action, CAPA, investigation, or submitted documentation for a numbered CGMP violation. Attach to the existing violation_N atom; do not encode the full critique paragraph or underlying detail value.

Preserves a response/CAPA critique with its numbered CGMP violation and CGMP citation family before deterministic projection to fda_response_assessment/5. Projection is valid only when cgmps_citation agrees with the typed fda_cgmp_violation_item/5 bundle.

Preserves source-stated missing response documentation, protocol, record, evidence, or validation material before deterministic projection to fda_response_assessment/5 with documentation_not_provided.
Preserves source-stated failure-to-investigate response critiques before deterministic projection to fda_response_assessment/5 with not_investigated.

Preserves one numbered insanitary-condition observation, distinct from the CGMP violation list. Use compact categories only: sterility_assurance for aseptic/contact practices; airflow_control for first-air, smoke-study, or airflow failures.
States a compact source-stated adulteration basis and authority without explanatory prose. authority_or_scope is closed to FDCA adulteration authority atoms such as fdca_501_a_2_a or fdca_501_a_2_b.

Preserves one response action, deadline, channel, and compact consequence or purpose. Use electronic_submission for source-stated email/mailbox/portal response channels; use issuing_office only when no electronic destination is stated.
Preserves a source-stated consultant recommendation without turning it into a full paragraph slot.
Preserves one compact conclusion-scope statement. Detailed value-boundary rules come from the registered carrier contract.
Records expected carrier absence or uncertainty. It is not an answer substitute.
